title: Level 800
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 800: "The Liminal Cable Car"}}{{Sky Theme}}{{Audio|file=Thinking_Space_2.mp3|volume=2|loop=yes|align=center}}{{Class Undetermined}}Level 800, more notably known as The Liminal Cable Car, is the 801st level of [[The Backrooms]]. == Description == {{Blurimage|file=Level 268...png|caption=A recreation of the outside of '''Level 800''' by Bechir Al-Laid.}}Level 800 is an endless network of cable cars suspended by numerous aberrant cables, usually featuring unnatural, distorted architectural designs. These cables move in ascending, descending, or horizontal directions, often parallel or diverging from nearby ones. The cables can also connect to one another, creating other pathways for cable cars to diverge into. Due to the level’s confusing structure and overall nature, meeting other wanderers is extremely rare, unless two people enter from the same location. The landscape of Level 800 consists mostly of grassy mountains and flatlands, affected by a phenomenon that causes any physical object to phase through the intangible landscape. Naturally occurring floating islands—small oases that contain flourishing plant life that appear rarely—sometimes house items such as [[Object 1|Almond Water]], food, and tools that are occasionally rusted. The level's sky is permanently daytime, though Smilers can still spawn in the shade and enclosed spaces of the cable cars. The weather in this level is similar to [[The Frontrooms]], with natural weather such as clouds or storms usually occurring every so often. The level's environment also includes multiple signs that point to a possible exit to [[The Frontrooms]].These signs appear briefly, often seen as areas that almost resemble towns. They're deemed by M.E.G. as just regular hallucinations/phenomena and recommend that they should be ignored. These signs are often misleading, causing one to venture deep into the level or into a [[Level 398|false reality]]. == Cable Cars == The cable cars are almost identical to a regular Frontrooms cable car. They are mostly composed of metals, glass, and various wallpapers, most commonly being similar to [[Level 0]], though they may come in other designs and variants. They often vary in size, ranging from small enclosed spaces to large, open interiors. The interior of a cable car often has transparent, fragile doors; chairs that may periodically shift; and potentially hostile [[Entity 2|Windows]]. Cable cars may occasionally include supplies such as toys, [[Object 1|Almond Water]]Almond Water that spawn within cable cars have a possibility to harbor any radioactive substances, so it is recommended to check before consumption., rusted weapons, packaged foods, and bottles of [[Object 12|Liquid Pain]]. These cable cars also include a button to choose a pathRecommended to use when the cables fork into 2 paths.. Though the buttons are, in some cases, inoperative, usually within damaged cable cars.{{Blurimage|file=Level-870-1.jpg|caption=The inside of a cable car. Photographer is unknown.}}Cable cars can often suffer from various events, likely affected by other phenomena. For instance, the cable car may shift into different sizes, have its properties altered,For example, the cable car shifts it's color scheme, slowly deteriorate, et-cetera. or even collapse abruptly. Furthermore, due to the odd properties of the level, the movement of the cable cars can often shift, traveling at different speeds or reversing their movement, which is fairly common. It is also noted that cable cars within this level may rarely depart out of bounds, particularly either drifting from their cable or ascending without a connection to their initial cable. The rarest phenomena that have occurred regarding them are instances of cables and cable cars merging or splitting. Even though crashes can often happen while inside a cable car, they are the safest method of transportation and supplies, as supplies such as food and [[Object 1|Almond Water]] can be found in most of them. However, it is important to first check whether the cable car appears dark inside; if so, it is strongly advised not to enter, as [[Entity 3|Smilers]] can frequently spawn in these cable cars. == Stations == Stations are rare structures that can be found within Level 800, accessed via cable cars. These stations are often seen either floating or stationed on the ground, acting as stable platforms for departing cable cars. These stations appear to include a lone glass platform, usually slightly damaged in some way, walls with different properties each, and sometimes a roof, with rare instances of liquid, such as blood or water, dripping from the roof, which creates small puddles. Some stations can occasionally harbor entities, so it is recommended to check before entering the station. Most of the stations contain areas resembling abandoned stores, which rarely contain items such as [[Object 1|Almond Water]] or packaged goods. Some of these stations have also been reported to have vending machines, having foods or drinks that appear to be from either unknown brands or originating from the Frontrooms. Rare phenomena can often occur within stations, occurring at random. For instance, flickering neon lights can often appear within the station that indicate a train is arriving. The train, upon entry, when departing from the station, transports wanderers to [[Level 154]]. Other phenomena that can affect the station include: parts of the station being non-collidable; certain intervals that cause the station to disappear and reappear briefly; and the entire station falling abruptly, presumably entering [[The Void]]. The best bet for one looking to noclip out of the level is through the stations, as they have the most well-known and consistent exits from the level, as most other exits are either unconfirmed, difficult to access, or theorized to lead straight to the void. == Hazards == Various hazards are present within the level, and can pose a risk to any wanderer who is currently present within Level 800. For instance, falling from the sky due to any sort of phenomenon, such as unobstruction, may lead to being transported to [[The Void]]. Other hazards, such as cable cars crashing into each other, cables separating or breaking, or random parts being unobstructed, can pose a huge risk to the wanderer, in many cases ending up with the wanderer and the cable car falling into [[The Void]]. Another hazard of this level includes the multitude of entities, which often nest in cable cars. Psychological hazards are also fairly common, which occur if staying on this level for extended periods of time. These quite often appear as hallucinations, traumatic events, or indescribable sounds capable of harming one's mental stability, ultimately resulting in traumatic recalls (PTSD) or even incurable insanity in extreme cases. The danger is also present in many of the aforementioned events, which amplify the previous hazards and further make this level harder to document. The [[M.E.G.]] has conducted several experiments to identify more hazards of the level over recent days, though it is currently halted due to its dangerous nature. {{Blurimage|file=Hi my name is Isak Hien and I am part of the Swedish team.jpg|caption=An photograph of '''Level 800''' taken by a drone. Photographer identified as Aníbal Rooney.}} == Entities == Entities are often sighted roaming around the level or hiding in specific areas within the level, usually within in cable cars or stations. Due to the recent discovery and hazardous nature of the level, many entities are unconfirmed and are yet to be discovered. Some confirmed entities include [[Entity 3|Smilers]] and [[Entity 10|Skin-Stealers]], though only in darker cable cars, as they are significantly darker than the rest of the level. Other entities also nest within the cable cars, often staying at one place, such as [[Entity 4|Deathmoths]] and [[Entity 2|Windows]]. There are also [[Phenomenon 35|victims of the Wretched Cycle]] present; however, they are rarely sighted due to the rare chance of meeting other wanderers, also affecting Wretches. There have also been reported sightings of shadowy figures, but these are at the present moment unconfirmed and are presumed to be other humans that are in darker cable cars. == Colonies and Outposts == Due to the nature of the level, no colonies exist in the present moment. However, there have been attempts to establish small, temporary research colonies by the [[M.E.G.|M.E.G]]. == Entrances and Exits == === Entrances === * Finding a ticket in [[Level 610]] or [[Level 711]] and redeeming it at a grey dispenser, Level 610 and Level 711 are the only two confirmed levels that contain the grey dispenser. causing one to pass out and noclip into this level. * Alternatively, one may enter this level by getting between -81 and -100 karma in [[Level 995]]. === Exits === * Falling down and phasing through the landscape will transport you to [[The Void]]. * Noclipping at a station will transport you to [[Level 55]], [[Level 305]], or [[Level 167]]. However, if one noclips on a vending machine, one will instead be sent to [[Level 610]]. * Boarding a train at a station that has neon flickering lights will take you to [[Level 154]]. This exit is one-way and the wanderer cannot return back to this level. * Entering a dark cable car leads to [[Level 6]]. * Wanderers traveling on this level, may find that exiting a car during an abrupt stop places them at the top of a stairwell leading into [[Level 825]]. {{Footnotes}}{{Collapse|initial=> Open Author & Licensing Information|final=< Close Author & Licensing Information|body=Authors: [[User:Spaceukfan|spaceukfan]] and [[User:E271 828182846|E271]] Writing style inspired by [[User:Codred23|Codred]]
